message:
fix(parser): tolerate trailing semicolons in flowchart direction headers

The `parse_graph_direction` helper in
`crates/fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs` uppercased each whitespace-
separated token from a flowchart/graph header and compared it against
the literal direction strings (`LR`/`RL`/`TB`/`TD`/`BT`). Real Mermaid
sources frequently end header lines with a trailing `;` separator
(e.g. `flowchart LR;` or `direction RL;`), which produced tokens like
`LR;` that never matched any arm. The parser then silently fell back
to the default top-to-bottom direction, losing the author's intent.

Fix: before comparing, slice off any non-alphabetic suffix by taking
the leading `is_ascii_alphabetic()` prefix of the token. Empty
prefixes (pure punctuation, numbers) `continue` so they can't match
accidentally. This leaves every existing case untouched (`LR` → `LR`,
`top-to-bottom` → no match as before) while correctly handling the
semicolon-terminated forms.

Also adds `flowchart_direction_parses_trailing_semicolons` under
`mod tests` which exercises both `flowchart LR;` at the header and
`direction RL;` on a dedicated line, asserting that both
`ir.direction` and `ir.meta.direction` end up set to the requested
value instead of the default.

Net diff: +19/-1 in mermaid_parser.rs.
